Smart Recommendations AI

📈 Increase Bid Opportunity

"scentrade essential oils" has 5.8% CVR with ACOS at 17.2% — well below your 25% target. A 15% bid increase could capture +23% more impressions.

🚫 Negative KW Suggestion

"free sample essential oil" consumed $42 with 0 conversions across 3 campaigns. Adding as negative could save ~$180/month.

💰 Budget Reallocation

Campaign NBH1-SP-PB01 is 92% budget consumed at noon. 2 under-spending campaigns have $18 available. Reallocate to prevent mid-day budget cap?

Group + Per-ASIN Configuration
Set category-level competitors (shared) and variant-level competitors (per-ASIN) for each group. Override settings as needed.
Tier 1 — Category Competitors (shared across group)
Auto-suggested competitors based on product type, category, and price band. Review and edit as needed.
Auto-populated
Campaign Skeleton (override defaults)
NBH1-SPKW-PB01-US-S-{ASIN}-E-KW
Campaign names are system-generated and not editable
Tier 2 — Variant-Level Competitors & Per-ASIN Overrides
Each ASIN inherits category competitors (Tier 1). Add variant-specific competitors below. Override Target ACOS or bid ceiling individually.

KW Research Approval Mode
Choose whether to auto-approve keyword research results or manually review them before campaign creation. When auto-approve is OFF, you can review each ASIN's keywords on the portal, make changes, then bulk-approve all ASINs in the Review Hub (Stage 4).
OFF — Manual Review
Manual Review mode: After processing, all ASINs will appear in the Review Hub where you can review keyword research results on the portal, make modifications, then approve individually or in bulk via spreadsheet export/import.
Async Parallel Processing
Keyword research and campaign building runs in the background for all ASINs simultaneously. Review progress below.

ASIN Config Pre-conditions Complete all Required items before proceeding
ASIN found in ASIN Config Required
Listing Status = Eligible Required
Target ACOS % Required
Suggested range for your product: 25–40%
Target ACOS = ad spend as % of ad revenue. Kepler uses this to calculate bids: Bid = Price × ACOS × CVR. Lower = more profitable but fewer impressions. Typical range for established products: 25–40%.
Competitor ASINs ≥ 5 — collected in Step 2 Required
Daily Budget ℹ $ Conditional
ASIN Status = On Required
Auto Pacing Conditional
What Auto Pacing does:
When enabled, Kepler automatically enables/disables campaigns based on daily performance targets. Campaigns may be paused mid-day if spend is ahead of pace, or re-enabled if behind. This ensures your daily budget is evenly distributed across the day for consistent visibility.
Bid Ceiling ℹ $ Optional

Identify Competitors

Add 5–9 competitor ASINs. These are used for keyword research and campaign targeting. You can add manually or let AI discover them.

Competitors selected
Minimum 5 required to proceed
5 min · 9 max

Rank attributes 1–7 to prioritize keyword grouping. Lower rank number = higher priority in campaign targeting structure. Rank 1 attributes generate the most keyword volume.

Campaign Config

Review auto-generated campaigns. Set Target ACOS, Daily Budget, and Status per campaign. Setting Status = ENABLED creates them live on Amazon.

Note: Campaigns shown below are auto-generated based on your approved keyword groups from Step 4. Match type strategy (Exact/Phrase/Broad/Auto) sets initial status. Fine-tune ACOS and budgets for each campaign before proceeding.

Checked = ENABLED · Unchecked = PAUSED
